    SortedNums = lists:sort(Nums),
    V = lists:remove_duplicates(SortedNums),

    % 2. Sort V descendingly
    V_sorted = lists:reverse(V),

    % 3. Initialize R: R is an association list {Value, Initial_Min_Reachable}
    % Initial_Min_Reachable[v] = v
    R_init = lists:map(fun(v) -> {v, v} end, V),

    % 4. Apply relaxation (Transitive closure minimization)
    R_final = lists:foldl(fun(R, X) ->
        % X is the current value being processed (descending order)
        
        % Find all proper divisors Y of X that are present in V
        Divisors = find_divisors(X, V),
        
        % Current minimum reachable value for X is R[X] (which is X initially)
        CurrentRX = lookup(X, R),
        
        % Relax R[X] using R[Y] for all divisors Y
        NewRX = lists:foldl(fun(MinRx, Y) ->
            % Y is a proper divisor of X
            Ry = lookup(Y, R),
            lists:min(MinRx, Ry)
        end, CurrentRX, Divisors),
        
        % Update R: replace {X, old_Rx} with {X, NewRx}
        lists:replace(R, X, {X, NewRX})
    end, R_init, V_sorted),

    % 5. Calculate the final minimum element sum.
    TotalSum = lists:foldl(fun(Sum, Num) ->
        % Find the minimum reachable value m*_Num
        MinReachable = lookup(Num, R_final),
        Sum + MinReachable
    end, 0, Nums).

%% Helper functions ----------------------------------------------------------

% Custom lookup function for association list (R)
lookup(Key, List) ->
    case lists:keyfind(Key, 1, List) of
        {Key, Value} -> Value;
        _ -> error(unknown_key) % Should not happen if initialization is correct
    end.

% Custom replacement function for association list (R)
lists:replace(List, Key, NewPair) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Item) ->
        case Item of
            {K, _} when K == Key -> NewPair;
            _ -> [Item | Acc]
        end
    end, [], List).

% Custom duplicate removal function
lists:remove_duplicates(List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Item) ->
        case lists:last(Acc) of
            Item2 when Item2 == Item -> Acc;
            _ -> [Item | Acc]
        end
    end, [], lists:reverse(List)).


% find_divisors(X, V) returns list of y in V such that y | X and y < X
find_divisors(X, V) ->

    lists:filter(fun(y) -> y < X end, Divisors).

    SortedNums = lists:sort(Nums),
    V = lists:remove_duplicates(SortedNums),

    V_sorted = lists:reverse(V),

    % 3. Initialize R: R is an association list {Value, Initial_Min_Reachable}
    % Initial_Min_Reachable[v] = v
    R_init = lists:map(fun(v) -> {v, v} end, V),

    % 4. Apply relaxation (Transitive closure minimization)
    R_final = lists:foldl(fun(R, X) ->
        % X is the current value being processed (descending order)
        
        % Find all proper divisors Y of X that are present in V
        Divisors = find_divisors(X, V),
        
        % Current minimum reachable value for X is R[X] (which is X initially)
        CurrentRX = lookup(X, R),
        
        % Relax R[X] using R[Y] for all divisors Y
        NewRX = lists:foldl(fun(MinRx, Y) ->
            % Y is a proper divisor of X
            Ry = lookup(Y, R),
            lists:min(MinRx, Ry)
        end, CurrentRX, Divisors),
        
        % Update R: replace {X, old_Rx} with {X, NewRx}
        lists:replace(R, X, {X, NewRX})
    end, R_init, V_sorted),

    % 5. Calculate the final minimum element sum.
    TotalSum = lists:foldl(fun(Sum, Num) ->
        % Find the minimum reachable value m*_Num
        MinReachable = lookup(Num, R_final),
        Sum + MinReachable
    end, 0, Nums).

%% Helper functions ----------------------------------------------------------

% Custom lookup function for association list (R)
lookup(Key, List) ->
    case lists:keyfind(Key, 1, List) of
        {Key, Value} -> Value;
        _ -> error(unknown_key) % Should not happen if initialization is correct
    end.

% Custom replacement function for association list (R)
lists:replace(List, Key, NewPair) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Item) ->
        case Item of
            {K, _} when K == Key -> NewPair;
            _ -> [Item | Acc]
        end
    end, [], List).

% Custom duplicate removal function
lists:remove_duplicates(List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Item) ->
        case lists:last(Acc) of
            Item2 when Item2 == Item -> Acc;
            _ -> [Item | Acc]
        end
    end, [], lists:reverse(List)).


% find_divisors(X, V) returns list of y in V such that y | X and y < X
find_divisors(X, V) ->
    Divisors = lists:filter(fun(y) -> mod(X, y) == 0 end, V),
    lists:filter(fun(y) -> y < X end, Divisors).

solve(Nums) ->
    % 1. Get unique values V and sort descendingly.
    V = unique_sorted_desc(Nums),

    % 2. Initialize R: R is a map: Value -> Minimum_Reachable_Value
    % Initialize R[v] = v for all v in V.
    R_init = maps:new(),
    lists:foldl(fun(Map, V_i) -> maps:put(V_i, V_i, Map) end, R_init, V),

    % 3. Apply relaxation (Iterate over V descendingly)
    R_final = lists:foldl(fun(Acc_R, X) ->
        update_R(Acc_R, X)
    end, R_init, V),

    % 4. Calculate the final minimum element sum.
    TotalSum = lists:foldl(fun(Acc_Sum, N_i) ->
        case maps:get(N_i, R_final) of
            SomeValue when is_integer(SomeValue) -> Acc_Sum + SomeValue;
            _ -> Acc_Sum % Should not happen if input Nums are valid
        end
    end, 0, Nums),

    TotalSum.

% Helper: Find unique elements and sort them descendingly
unique_sorted_desc(List) ->
    % Use maps to enforce uniqueness
    UniqueMap = maps:new(),
    lists:foldl(fun(Map, X) -> maps:put(X, true, Map) end, UniqueMap, List),
    UniqueList = maps:keys(UniqueMap),
    % Sort descendingly using a comparison function (B - A)
    lists:sort(fun(A, B) -> B - A end, UniqueList).

% Helper: Update R[X] based on its divisors Y in V.
% R_Map is the state of R before processing X.
update_R(R_Map, X) ->
    % Initial minimum for R[X] is X itself.
    X_Current_R = maps:get(X, R_Map, X),
    
    % Find the minimum reachable value by checking all divisors Y of X that are in V.
    Min_R_X = divisor_check_min(X, 1, X_Current_R, R_Map),
    
    % Update the map with the finalized R[X]
    maps:put(X, Min_R_X, R_Map).

% Recursive helper for finding divisors and minimizing R[X]
divisor_check_min(X, Y, Current_Min_R, R_Map) when Y * Y <= X ->
    
    % 1. Check divisor Y
    case maps:get(Y, R_Map) of
        SomeRy when is_integer(SomeRy) ->
            New_Min_R = min(Current_Min_R, SomeRy),
            
            % 2. Check conjugate Z = X/Y
            Z = X div Y,
            
            Case_Z = maps:get(Z, R_Map),
            New_Min_R2 = case Case_Z of
                SomeRz when is_integer(SomeRz) -> min(New_Min_R, SomeRz);
                _ -> New_Min_R
            end,
            
            % Continue checking next divisor Y+1
            divisor_check_min(X, Y + 1, New_Min_R2, R_Map);
        _ ->
            % Y is not in V, proceed to Y+1
            divisor_check_min(X, Y + 1, Current_Min_R, R_Map)
    end;

% Base case: Y*Y > X. Return the minimum found so far.
divisor_check_min(_, _, Current_Min_R, _R_Map) ->
    Current_Min_R.

    % 1. Get unique values V
    UniqueNums = lists:map(fun(X) -> X end, nums),
    UniqueNumsSorted = lists:sort(UniqueNums),
    V = lists:lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        case lists:last(Acc) of
            Last when Last == X -> Acc;
            _ -> [X | Acc]
        end
    end, [], UniqueNumsSorted),
    
    % Reverse V to get descending order for DP processing
    VDesc = lists:reverse(V),
    
    % 2. Initialize R (Map: value -> minimum reachable value)
    R0 = maps:fold(fun(X) -> {X, X} end, #{})(V),
    
    % 3. Apply relaxation (Transitive closure minimization)
    % Fold over VDesc, accumulating the map R
    R_final = lists:foldl(fun(R, X) ->
        % X is the current value being processed. R is the current state map.
        
        % Find divisors d of X present in V, such that d < X.
        % We iterate through potential divisors up to sqrt(X)
        
        MinReachable =

                if 0 is mod(X, Y) ->
                    % Y is a divisor
                    Min_Y = maps:get(Y, R, X), % Should exist since Y in V
                    Min_Z = 
                        if X rem Y == 0 ->
                            Z = X div Y,
                            if Z < X and maps:get(Z, R, X) =:= Z -> maps:get(Z, R, X)
                            else X
                        else X end,
                    
                    Min_Candidate = min(Min_Y, Min_Z),
                    min(Min, Min_Candidate)
                else
                    Min
            end, X, lists:sublist(1, floor(sqrt(X)))), % Initial min is X itself
            
        % Update R[X] with the calculated minimum
        maps:put(X, MinReachable, R)
    end, R0, VDesc),
    
    % 4. Calculate the final minimum element sum.
    TotalSum = lists:foldl(fun(Sum, N) ->
        % Look up the minimum reachable value R[N]
        MinReachable = maps:get(N, R_final, N),
        Sum + MinReachable
    end, 0, nums).

solve(nums) ->
    % 1. Get unique values V
    UniqueNums = lists:map(fun(X) -> X end, nums),
    UniqueNumsSorted = lists:sort(UniqueNums),
    V = lists:lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        case lists:last(Acc) of
            Last when Last == X -> Acc;
            _ -> [X | Acc]
        end
    end, [], UniqueNumsSorted),
    
    % Reverse V to get descending order for DP processing
    VDesc = lists:reverse(V),
    
    % 2. Initialize R (Map: value -> minimum reachable value)
    R0 = maps:fold(fun(X) -> {X, X} end, #{})(V),
    
    % 3. Apply relaxation (Transitive closure minimization)
    % Fold over VDesc, accumulating the map R
    R_final = lists:foldl(fun(R, X) ->
        % X is the current value being processed. R is the current state map.
        
        % Find divisors d of X present in V, such that d < X.
        % We iterate through potential divisors up to sqrt(X)
        
        MinReachable =
            lists:foldl(fun(Min, Y) ->
                if 0 is mod(X, Y) ->
                    % Y is a divisor
                    Min_Y = maps:get(Y, R, X), % Should exist since Y in V
                    Min_Z = 
                        if X rem Y == 0 ->
                            Z = X div Y,
                            if Z < X and maps:get(Z, R, X) =:= Z -> maps:get(Z, R, X)

                    Min_Candidate = min(Min_Y, Min_Z),
                    min(Min, Min_Candidate)

                    Min
            end, X, lists:sublist(1, floor(sqrt(X)))), % Initial min is X itself
            
        % Update R[X] with the calculated minimum
        maps:put(X, MinReachable, R)
    end, R0, VDesc),
    
    % 4. Calculate the final minimum element sum.
    TotalSum = lists:foldl(fun(Sum, N) ->
        % Look up the minimum reachable value R[N]
        MinReachable = maps:get(N, R_final, N),
        Sum + MinReachable
    end, 0, nums).

    TotalSum.
```
